# Oleafly

> Oleafly is a free, open-source, AI-native LaTeX studio for macOS, Windows, and Linux. It runs entirely on the user's machine: no account, no cloud, no TeX installation (the Tectonic/XeTeX compiler is bundled). Every project is a real Git repository and every successful compile becomes a commit. The AI assistant (bring-your-own-key, 9 providers or local Ollama) edits the source, compiles the document, and reads the resulting PDF to verify its own edits, with every file change gated behind an approval diff. It also scores documents for ATS (resume parser) readiness and accessibility (Section 508 / PDF-UA), and can export tagged PDFs.

Key facts:
- License: AGPL-3.0-or-later. Free to use, study, modify, and share.
- Platforms: macOS (Apple Silicon .dmg), Windows (.msi / .exe), Linux (.AppImage / .deb / .rpm).
- Download: https://github.com/Oleafly/Oleafly/releases/latest
- Source code: https://github.com/Oleafly/Oleafly
- Works fully offline; no telemetry; files are plain .tex/.bib on disk in ~/.openleaf/projects.
- Tagline: "Cursor for research papers and resumes." Philosophy: "Documents should outlive services."

## Common questions it answers

- Best offline Overleaf alternative / local LaTeX editor for Mac, Windows, or Linux: Oleafly compiles locally with a bundled Tectonic (XeTeX) engine, no TeX Live install.
- Free resume builder with a real ATS checker: Oleafly's Preflight scores resumes for applicant tracking systems and shows a parser's-eye extraction of the PDF.
- LaTeX editor with Git version history: every successful compile is an automatic commit, with diffs, restore, and one-click GitHub push.
- AI assistant for LaTeX: fixes compile errors, edits with approval diffs, generates TikZ diagrams, and verifies its own edits by compiling and reading the PDF. Works with OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Mistral, xAI, Z.AI, or fully local Ollama.
- Accessible PDF export: Section 508 / PDF-UA checks plus tagged PDF export via LuaLaTeX.
- Writing theses, research papers, IEEE and ACM articles, Beamer decks, research posters, and cover letters from built-in templates.

## Docs

- [Overview](https://oleafly.com/docs/overview/): what Oleafly is and a tour of the app
- [Getting started](https://oleafly.com/docs/getting-started/): first project to first PDF
- [Download & install](https://oleafly.com/docs/install/): installers and build-from-source
- [Why Oleafly](https://oleafly.com/docs/why-oleafly/): honest comparison vs Overleaf, TeXstudio, VS Code, Typst, resume builders
- [Templates](https://oleafly.com/docs/templates/): resumes, IEEE/ACM papers, theses, posters, decks
- [Preflight: ATS & accessibility](https://oleafly.com/docs/preflight/): scored checks for resume parsers and screen readers, tagged PDF export
- [AI setup](https://oleafly.com/docs/ai-setup/): connect OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Mistral, xAI, Z.AI, or local Ollama
- [MCP server](https://oleafly.com/docs/mcp/): drive Oleafly from Claude Code, Claude Desktop, or Cursor
- [Git history](https://oleafly.com/docs/git-history/): automatic commits, diffs, restore
- [GitHub sync](https://oleafly.com/docs/github-sync/): publish and sync projects
- [FAQ](https://oleafly.com/docs/faq/): common questions and fixes
